title: in-toto Layout
description: Schema for the in-toto Layout metadata document, which defines the expected steps of a software supply chain, the authorized functionaries for each step, and the inspection rules used during verification. A signed Layout is the root of trust for an in-toto supply chain.

_type.description: Identifies this metadata document as an in-toto layout.

expires.description: ISO 8601 date-time string after which this layout is considered expired and verification must fail.

readme.description: Human-readable description of the software supply chain and the purpose of this layout.

keys.description: Dictionary of public keys authorized to sign link metadata for steps in this layout. Keys are key IDs (fingerprints); values are key objects.

steps.description: Ordered list of steps that must be performed in the software supply chain. Each step specifies who may perform it and what artifact rules must hold.

inspect.description: List of inspection commands to run during verification. Inspections compute link metadata locally and can apply artifact rules against the combined set of supply chain artifacts.

Key.description: A public key used to verify signatures on link metadata or on the layout itself.

scheme.description: Signing scheme used with this key, e.g. rsassa-pss-sha256 or ed25519.

keyid_hash_algorithms.description: Hash algorithms used to compute the key ID.

keyval.description: Key material. For asymmetric keys, contains a 'public' property with the PEM-encoded public key.

Step.description: A single step in the software supply chain. Specifies who is authorized to perform the step and the artifact rules governing materials and products.

name.description: Unique name identifying this step within the layout. Must match the name used in the corresponding link metadata.

pubkeys.description: List of key IDs of functionaries authorized to perform this step and sign its link metadata.

expected_command.description: Optional list of command tokens expected to be run for this step. Verification may warn if the actual command differs.

expected_materials.description: Artifact rules applied to materials recorded in this step's link metadata.

expected_products.description: Artifact rules applied to products recorded in this step's link metadata.

threshold.description: Minimum number of valid link metadata files required from different functionaries for this step to be considered complete.

Inspection.description: A verification-time command that is run by the verifier to produce link metadata locally. Allows applying artifact rules over the final supply chain state.

name.description: Unique name identifying this inspection within the layout.

run.description: Command tokens to execute during the inspection phase of verification.

expected_materials.description: Artifact rules applied to materials recorded by this inspection.

expected_products.description: Artifact rules applied to products recorded by this inspection.

ArtifactRule.description: A rule that constrains which artifacts (materials or products) are acceptable for a step. Rules are expressed as arrays of tokens following the in-toto artifact rule grammar, e.g. ['MATCH', 'foo', 'WITH', 'PRODUCTS', 'FROM', 'build'].
